nodes.name: Daily 8AM IST
nodes.notes: Runs daily at 08:00. Workflow timezone is Asia/Kolkata (see Settings), so this fires at 08:00 IST.

nodes.name: Fetch Daily Summaries
nodes.notes: GET /api/orders/daily-summary (internal auth). Returns { summaries: [...], meta } ACROSS ALL ORGS. The endpoint does all date math (IST), fetches yesterday's orders, today's in-production orders, and overdue invoices, returns STRUCTURED fields (date_label, counts, formatted ₹ values) + owner phone + language_preference, and ALREADY drops orgs that opted out (whatsapp_proactive_enabled=false), have no owner phone, or have no data — so each item here is ready to map into the template.

nodes.name: Split Into Summaries
nodes.notes: Fans the summaries array out to one item per org. This is the last node whose OUTPUT carries full summary fields (organization_id, owner_phone, language_preference, structured template fields) and is 1:1 with the source, so downstream nodes reference it by name for item-stable lineage.

nodes.name: Has Data
nodes.notes: Defense-in-depth guard for spec rule 5 ('only send if there's actual data'), the 'must have an owner phone' rule, and a non-empty language_preference (so template selection never falls through to a null locale). The endpoint already enforces all three (it filters whatsapp_proactive_enabled, drops no-owner/no-data orgs, and defaults locale to 'en'); this ensures we never send an empty or mis-localised summary even if the contract changes.

nodes.name: Send Daily Summary

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ to: $json.owner_phone, type: 'template', orgId: $json.organization_id, template: { name: $json.language_preference === 'gu' ? 'daily_morning_summary_gujarati' : 'daily_morning_summary', language: { code: $json.language_preference === 'gu' ? 'gu' : 'en' }, components: [{ type: 'body', parameters: [{ type: 'text', text: String($json.date_label) }, { type: 'text', text: String($json.yesterday_order_count) }, { type: 'text', text: String($json.yesterday_value_formatted) }, { type: 'text', text: String($json.production_count) }, { type: 'text', text: String($json.overdue_count) }, { type: 'text', text: String($json.overdue_total_formatted) }] }] } }) }}

nodes.notes: POST /api/whatsapp/send (internal auth) → Meta Cloud API. Sends the Meta-approved daily_morning_summary template (Utility category, delivers outside the 24h window) to the org OWNER's WhatsApp number — never a customer chat (Rule A). Maps the endpoint's six structured fields into body variables {{1}}–{{6}}: date_label, yesterday_order_count, yesterday_value_formatted, production_count, overdue_count, overdue_total_formatted. Picks the _gujarati template + gu language code when language_preference === 'gu', else English.

nodes.name: Log Summary Sent

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ /* the Send node returns only { sent, messageId }, so org + phone are read from Split — the last un-filtered 1:1 node — for index-stable paired-item lineage */ JSON.stringify({ orgId: $('Split Into Summaries').item.json.organization_id, chatPhone: $('Split Into Summaries').item.json.owner_phone, messageType: 'daily_summary_sent', timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }) }}

nodes.notes: POST /api/analytics/log-intent (internal auth) → PostHog. Privacy-safe: orgId + masked phone + event type only, never order counts or amounts (CLAUDE.md security rule #8). Reads org/phone from $('Split Into Summaries').item, not the Send response which strips them.

nodes.name: Error Trigger
nodes.notes: Set this workflow as its own Error Workflow in Settings > Error Workflow.

nodes.name: Log Error

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ source: 'n8n_daily_order_summary', error: $json.error?.message, workflow: $json.workflow?.name, node: $json.execution?.lastNodeExecuted, executionId: $json.execution?.id, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }) }}

nodes.notes: Logs failures to Sentry via /api/errors/log. Never messages a customer chat.
